title: Rail element for a camera slider rail system
abstract: A rail element (10, 33) for a camera slider system or, more generally, a tube joiner for a rail comprised of an expansion device (23, 30), located internally at an open end (12, 32) of a tubular body (11, 33). The expansion device is configured to fit within a male part or joiner element (16, 31) that may be inserted into the end of the tube (10, 33) and can be actuated to an expanded state where it exerts force radially outwards through the joiner (16, 31) to hold it against the open end (12, 32), thereby joining two tubular or rail element ends together.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A rail element for a camera slider system, comprising : a longitudinal body portion; an expandable joiner element, located at or proximate to at least one end of the longitudinal body portion wherein the expandable joiner element is or further comprises an expansion device, housed within the body portion, configured to exert force outwardly. 2. The rail element of claim 1, wherein the at least one end is an open tubular end for receiving the joiner element which extends therefrom for being received by an adjacent open tubular end of a rail element. 3. The rail element of claim 1, wherein the joiner element extends from the at least one end for being received by an adjacent open tubular end of a rail element. 4. The rail element of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the joiner element is a cylindrical length or insert. 5. The rail element of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the joiner element comprises a split in a sidewall thereof. 6. The rail element of claim 5, including a pin or lug protruding internally from the longitudinal body portion, to be accommodated by the split of the joiner element. 7. The rail element of any one of the preceding claims, wherein an external wall of the joiner element is corrugated and/or generally includes a plurality of longitudinal channels or features. 8. The rail element of claim 1, wherein the expansion device includes a lever for actuating a rod extending longitudinally within the longitudinal body portion. 9. The rail element of claim 8, wherein the rod extends through at least one resilient element and includes a widened head at a terminal end thereof. 10. The rail element of claim 9, wherein the lever is mounted from a block element, housed within the body portion, with a bore therethrough housing the rod. 11. The rail element of claim 10, wherein the lever is configured to causes the rod to move in a direction away from the at least one end of the longitudinal body portion, thereby compressing the resilient element between the widened head and a wall of the block element, causing radial expansion against the joiner element. 12. The rail element of any one of claims 8 to 11, wherein the lever is accessible at and/or through an outer wall of the body portion. 13. The rail element of claim 12, wherein the lever is configured to travel between an upstanding position, where the expansion device is in an unactivated state, to a position substantially flush with the outer wall of the body portion, where the expansion device is in an activated state. 14. The rail element of any one of claims 9 to 11, wherein there are at least two resilient elements, spaced apart by a non-resilient element. 15. The rail element of claim 14, wherein the non-resilient element has a diameter approximately the same as the resilient elements or at least slightly less than any tubular member into which it is to be inserted. 16. The rail element of any one of claims 1 or 8 to 15, wherein the expansion device is configured to exert an axial force as well as the outwardly exerted force. 17. The rail element of claim 16, wherein the axial force is a pull force for pulling two adjacent rail elements together. 18. The rail element of claim 1, wherein the expansion device provides an internally expandable member, relative to the body portion, configured to exert radial and/or axial force for securing one element against another. 19. The rail element of claim 18, wherein the expansion device is expandable by mechanical, hydraulic or chemical means that achieves expansion of the internal member against a surrounding element. 20. A rail system for a camera slider, incorporating at least one rail element according to any preceding claim. 21. The rail system of claim 20, further comprising an end support element. 22. The rail system of claim 21, wherein the end support element comprises a joiner element, of like dimension to the expandable joiner element, for being received by an open tubular end of the at least one rail element. 23. The rail system of claim 21 or 22, wherein the end support element comprises foldable and/or extensible legs for supporting the rail element above a ground surface. 24. The rail system of any one of claims 20 to 23, comprising at least a pair of rail elements arranged in parallel. 25. The rail system of claim 24, further comprising a camera slider locatable between the pair of rails. 26. A tube joiner for joining the ends of two tubes, the tube joiner being comprised of: an expansion device to be housed internally and proximate to an end of at least one of the tubes, the expansion device having a first, unexpanded, state configured to fit within a male part extending between the two tubes to be joined, and a second, expanded, state when it is configured to exert force internally against the male part to hold it against a female part that receives the male part. 27. The tube joiner of claim 22, wherein the expansion device includes a rod and a lever for actuating the rod which extends longitudinally within the tube. 28. The tube joiner of claim 23, wherein the rod extends through at least one resilient element and includes a widened head at a terminal end thereof. 29. The tube joiner of claim 24, wherein the lever is mounted from a block element, housed within the tube, with a bore therethrough housing the rod. 30. The tube joiner of claim 25, wherein, in the second state, the lever is configured to causes the rod to move in a direction away from the end of the tube, thereby compressing the resilient element between the widened head and a wall of the block element, causing radial expansion against the male part. 31. The tube joiner of any one of claims 23 to 26, wherein the lever is accessible at and/or through an outer wall of one or both of the tubes to be joined. 32. The tube joiner of claim 27, wherein the lever is configured to travel between an upstanding position, where the expansion device is in the first state, to a position substantially flush with the outer wall, where the expansion device is in the second state. 33. The tube joiner of any one of claims 28 to 30, wherein there are at least two resilient elements, spaced apart by a non-resilient element. 34. The tube joiner of claim 33, wherein the non-resilient element has a diameter approximately the same as the resilient elements or at least slightly less than a tubular member into which it is to be inserted. 35. The tube joiner of any one of claims 26 to 34, wherein the expansion device is configured to exert a radial and/or axial force. 36. The tube joiner of claim 35, wherein the force is an axial force that is a pull force for pulling two adjacent tubes together. 37. The tube joiner of claim 26, wherein the expansion device is expandable by mechanical, hydraulic or chemical means that achieves expansion of the male part against the surrounding female part. 38. A rail element or tube joiner substantially as herein described with reference to the accompanying drawings.
